Document:
# OpenCode Development Guide
This guide explains how to use OpenCode for developing OpenEdge 14.
## What is OpenCode?
OpenCode is an AI coding assistant similar to Claude Code, but with some unique features:
- [bold]Background Processes[/bold]: Run server and long tasks without blocking - [bold]Custom Commands[/bold]: Slash commands for common tasks - [bold]Skills[/bold]: Automated workflows for specific tasks
## Installing OpenCode
### 1. Install OpenCode
[code]npm install -g opencode[/code]
Or download from [bold]https://opencode.ai[/bold]
### 2. Configure Plugins
Add to [code]~/.config/opencode/opencode.json[/code]:
[code]{ "plugin": [ "@howaboua/opencode-background-process@latest", "@jredeker/opencode-morph-fast-apply@latest" ] }[/code]
## Starting a Development Session
[code]cd /home/rusted/Git/OpenEdge-14 opencode[/code]
OpenCode will automatically read CLAUDE.md and .ai/ files for context.
## Available Commands
| Command | Description | |---------|-------------| | /build | Build project (Release) | | /server | Start game server | | /kill-server | Stop game server | | /doctor | Run diagnostics | | /review | Review code for bugs | | /plan | Switch to plan mode |
## Running the Server
### Option 1: Background (Recommended)
In OpenCode, simply say: [bold]"start server"[/bold]
The server will run in background without blocking your conversation.
### Option 2: Manual Terminal
[code]bash runserver.sh[/code]
## Checking Server Status
In OpenCode, use:
- [bold]"show server logs"[/bold] - View recent server output - [bold]"check server status"[/bold] - See if server is running - [bold]"stop server"[/bold] - Kill the running server
## Tips for Development
1. [bold]Use Plan Mode[/bold] for complex tasks - Press Shift+Tab 2. [bold]Use Background Processes[/bold] for server and builds 3. [bold]Check Logs[/bold] frequently when testing features 4. [bold]Run /build[/bold] before making commits 5. [bold]Use /review[/bold] before submitting changes
## Troubleshooting
### Server Won't Start
- Kill existing process: [code]killall Content.Server[/code] - Check port: [code]lsof -ti:1212 | xargs kill -9[/code]
### OpenCode Not Responding
- Press Escape to stop generation - Use /clear to reset conversation
### Plugin Not Working
- Restart OpenCode - Check plugins are in [code]~/.config/opencode/opencode.json[/code]
## Next Steps
- Read CLAUDE.md for project context - Check .ai/errors.md for known issues - Use /doctor to verify project health
